addressedReviewFindings.finding: No high or mid findings; design accepted.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Implementation plan traces accepted TASK-006 contract boundaries and no-side-effect constraints.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: No high or mid findings; TASK-006 implementation plan accepted.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Implemented additive Swift contracts and deterministic tests across RielaAddons, RielaEvents, RielaHook, RielaGraphQL, and RielaServer while preserving TypeScript/Bun fallback and no-side-effect constraints.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Step 7 review reported two mid findings: Swift package manifest metadata and validation parity gaps, and event validation parity gaps versus the TypeScript contracts.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Added TS-compatible manifest metadata fields, dependency string decoding, required metadata/checksum/add-on safety validation, file-change and s3-repository event contracts, template reference validation, and output destination validation with deterministic XCTest regressions.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Implementation author self-review found nested package manifest DTOs for add-on capabilities, workflow metadata, integrity metadata, and signatures could still ignore unsupported keys.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Added fail-closed unsupported-key decoding for those nested DTOs and deterministic decoder regressions.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Step 7 review reported incomplete add-on capability/capabilityGrant parity and dry-run input mapping that did not render TypeScript-compatible event templates.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Added capability name, defaultPolicy, sensitive reason, duplicate, scope, and dependency capabilityGrant validation. Added event-input/template dry-run rendering with event, source, and binding roots, exact-reference object preservation, array traversal, and deterministic regressions.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Step 7 review reported EventBindingContract omitted enabled and match rules, and hook event normalization omitted the full TypeScript HookEventName catalog.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Added EventBindingContract enabled and match rules with eventType, conversationId, and pathPrefix dry-run matching. Expanded hook event normalization to the full known TypeScript HookEventName catalog and added deterministic regressions.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Implementation author self-review found Swift synthesized Decodable would require enabled on event sources and bindings even though TypeScript event config treats enabled as optional.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Added custom decoding defaults for EventSourceContract.enabled and EventBindingContract.enabled, plus deterministic decoding regression.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Step 7 review found HookContext Codable decode required newly added non-optional fields, breaking pre-TASK-006 minimal JSON with only agentSessionId and optional agentBackend.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Added custom HookContext decoding defaults for vendor, eventName, workingDirectory, and backendMetadata, plus deterministic regression.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Step 7 review found EventBindingContract required workflowId instead of TypeScript workflowName, dry-run input mapping accepted unsupported merge fallback, and outputDestinations were omitted from runtime-variable evidence.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Added workflowName and execution-mode binding contracts, typed event-input/template input mapping decoding, workflowName validation exceptions for supervisor-dispatch and schedule-registration, outputDestinations dry-run runtime variables, and deterministic regressions.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Step 7 review found event dry-run runtime variables omitted humanInput and eventMailboxBridgePolicy, GraphQL schemaContract omitted DTO inspection fields and undefined ControlPlaneResult, and hook context resolution missed Riela env context plus invalid recording/capture validation.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Added humanInput mirroring and eventMailboxBridgePolicy runtime variables, expanded schemaContract and schema assertions, added Riela hook context resolver/env validation, and deterministic regressions.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Step 7 review found add-on manifest validation did not preserve TypeScript execution-artifact safety and EventBindingContract omitted authored mailboxBridge policy overrides.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Tightened add-on source/execution artifact path and descriptor validation, added authored EventMailboxBridgePolicy decoding and dry-run application, and added deterministic regressions.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Step 7 review found EventSourceContract missed TypeScript webhook/S3 keys, EventContractValidator omitted mailboxBridge consumer compatibility rules, and GraphQL envelope parsing did not trim or normalize query, operationName, and variables like TypeScript.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Added TypeScript-shaped webhook and nested S3 event source decode/encode support, mailboxBridge consumer mode validation, GraphQL query and operationName trimming plus variables null normalization, and deterministic regressions.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Step 7 review found event route conflict validation ignored effective S3/default HTTP paths, webhook and S3 required-field validation was incomplete, and server context sanitization leaked ambient workflow execution keys.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Added effective event HTTP path resolution for webhook and s3-repository sources, route conflict validation across explicit and default paths, required webhook path and S3 eventReceiver validation, ambient workflow environment stripping in ServerRequestContext, and deterministic regressions.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Step 7 adversarial review found workflow package manifest validation ignored packageRoot and accepted missing workflow.json bundles, and event HTTP path resolution omitted chat-sdk webhook routes from conflict detection.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Added non-destructive workflow.json file existence validation to FileWorkflowPackageManifestLoader.validate, added TypeScript-shaped chat-sdk webhook decode/encode and effective route conflict validation, and deterministic regressions.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Step 6 self-review confirmed chat-sdk webhook route parity and tightened missing webhook/path/auth validation before independent review.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Added hasChatWebhook tracking, required chat-sdk webhook/path/auth diagnostics, updated dry-run chat fixtures to use valid webhook contracts, and re-ran targeted and full Swift tests.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Step 7 adversarial review found HookParsing copied raw sensitive hook payloads into public HookContext.backendMetadata and server header normalization could trap on duplicate mixed-case headers.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Redacted parsed hook backendMetadata with the existing hook redaction policy, replaced header normalization with deterministic duplicate handling, and added focused regressions.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Step 7 review found chat-sdk event validation accepted missing or unsupported providers and did not reject binding match.eventType values unsupported by the TypeScript chat-sdk provider capability set.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Added Swift chat-sdk provider validation for the TypeScript provider set, provider event-type capability validation for binding match.eventType, and deterministic validation and dry-run regressions.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Step 7 review found chat-sdk capability validation checked only binding match.eventType while dry-run matching also honors top-level EventBindingContract.eventType.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Applied the same provider capability validation to top-level chat-sdk binding eventType, and added deterministic validation and dry-run regressions for unsupported top-level chat-sdk event types.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Step 7 adversarial review found FileWorkflowPackageManifestLoader accepted arbitrary URL schemes through Data(contentsOf:) and hook payload hashes used unsorted JSON encoder output.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Added deterministic non-file URL rejection before manifest reads, canonicalized hook payload hash encoding with sorted JSON keys, and added focused regressions.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Step 7 review found manifest decoding defaulted missing tags to [] without validation and normalizePackageRelativePath accepted raw .. segments such as a/.. and a/../b.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Added decoded tag-field tracking, tag validation for missing and empty entries, fail-closed raw .. path rejection, and focused regressions.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Step 6 self-review found explicit JSON null tags could still be accepted by decodeIfPresent despite comm-000071 requiring tags to be an array of strings.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Changed present tag fields to decode [String] directly and added top-level plus workflow metadata null-tag regressions.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Step 7 adversarial review found GraphQL schemaContract exposed continueSession without an input payload argument even though GraphQLContinueSessionRequest carries input.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Added ContinueSessionInput, changed continueSession to accept structured input, and added schema regressions.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Step 7 adversarial review found allowedBuiltins could authorize untrusted package add-ons with built-in names and normalizePackageRelativePath did not reject Windows absolute paths.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Required trusted source.builtin metadata together with allowedBuiltins for built-in resolution, rejected Windows absolute paths in general package-relative path normalization, and added focused regressions.
